verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 encodes the type 1 inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor, a large tetrameric calcium-release channel resident in the endoplasmic reticulum membrane. Its transmembrane segments face the ER lumen/cytoplasm, not the extracellular face of the plasma membrane. Pre-no checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs target the ITPR1 protein body extracellularly. (2) No ectodomain-shedding / TM-precursor biology documented. (3) No covalent or wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the PM outer leaflet. (4) No documented minority PM pool or secretory cycling to the cell surface. (5) No cancer/oncogenic, cell-death, germline-restricted, or activation-induced outer-leaflet display documented for this protein specifically. No CD designation. HGNC family is IP3 receptors, all ER-resident. Verdict: strictly endomembrane-resident.
